TechnologyStack.Framework: stdlib net/http plus database/sql pgx driver plus goose-style SQL migrations plus asynq plus React, React Router, Vite, and packet-local MCP/Telegram clients

ArchitectureShape.goal: Separate orchestration from execution while keeping a single local deployment topology.
ArchitectureShape.control-plane: Go API server owns job creation, state transitions, artifact metadata, job graph relationships, polling and WebSocket and webhook status delivery, idempotency, and cancel or retry semantics.
ArchitectureShape.execution-plane: Python workers own heavy execution through one transcription worker plus one generic agent-runner worker; batch aggregate transcription is handled by worker-transcription, and report generation and deep research are public operations backed by `agent_run`, not dedicated worker services.
ArchitectureShape.adapters: Telegram bot, Web UI, and MCP server remain thin adapters over the same HTTP API.
ArchitectureShape.batch-transcription-contract: Mixed-source batch transcription uses `POST /v1/transcription-jobs/batch`, a `batch-transcription.v1` source manifest with stable unique source labels, a root aggregate transcription job, per-source transcription children, and the `job:transcription.aggregate` task type for root artifact assembly.
ArchitectureShape.batch-draft-contract: Telegram pre-submit batch baskets are API-owned drafts persisted in PostgreSQL with draft_id, version, owner scope, ordered items, and status open/submitted/expired/canceled. Telegram callback data carries draft_id plus version or a draft-scoped item token; old `basket:*` callbacks are stale and never operate on a local current basket. Submit atomically promotes the draft into the existing batch transcription root/children/aggregate graph.
ArchitectureShape.telegram-i18n-contract: Telegram RU/EN localization is packet-local typed adapter infrastructure under `apps/telegram-bot/src/telegram_adapter/i18n`. The adapter resolves locale from Telegram user language metadata with RU default, localizes command registration, keyboards, source-label presentation, stale callback responses, access-denied copy, delayed basket summaries, media-group flushes, and report/deep-research flows, and does not persist locale into API-owned batch draft records or job semantics.
ArchitectureShape.future-agent-execution-contract: Approved architecture contract: autonomous AI-agent execution uses one generic `agent-runner` worker behind the existing control plane, dispatches harness implementations by `harness_name`, persists runs as the `agent_run` job family through the existing job tables, stores only normalized result summary plus redacted metadata and opaque request references in control-plane payloads, keeps full logs and large outputs as artifacts or private objects, supports first-iteration container-local Claude Code execution through generated Anthropic-compatible settings JSON, and exposes concurrency as explicit per-harness policy.

component.role: Go HTTP API and WebSocket server
Approved generic AI-agent worker that dispatches harness implementations by `harness_name` through the existing control plane and owns report/deep-research AI operation execution
React UI for job creation, monitoring, downloads, and admin actions
Thin Python Telegram adapter over HTTP API with packet-local typed RU/EN i18n and artifact-presentation logic
Thin MCP adapter over HTTP API
OpenAPI definitions, JSON schemas, WebSocket schemas, webhook schemas, and shared enums
Deferred shared TypeScript SDK; current Web UI and MCP adapter use packet-local clients
Deferred shared Python SDK; current Telegram adapter and Python workers use packet-local clients

MonorepoLayout.area: API-owned business orchestration and state transitions
React application shell, job features, and API client wiring
Packet-local typed Telegram locale keys, catalogs, validation, and localized command helpers
TypeScript MCP server entrypoints and tools
Generic agent-runner implementation for report and deep-research operations

MigrationConventions.naming-rule: Use a zero-padded numeric prefix plus a semantic snake_case suffix such as `0001_job_control_plane_foundation.sql`; phase-only filenames are not accepted.

AgentRunnerConfigSurface.job-family: `agent_run` is the approved control-plane family for generic agent execution and `agent_run_create` is the approved submission kind.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.control-plane-status: `agent_run_create` is implemented in contracts, HTTP, jobs, storage, and queue policy as a request-only root-job creation surface.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.worker-runtime-status: `workers/agent-runner` is implemented for the worker-local runtime, lease-policy, and compose-service slices: it claims `agent_run`, dispatches deterministic fixture harnesses by `harness_name`, resolves worker-only short-lived `request_access` for the private request envelope, runs first-iteration `claude-code` inside the container through generated Anthropic-compatible settings, writes `agent_result_json` and `execution_log` artifacts, enforces per-harness in-process lease limits, and finalizes through WorkerCommon. Live fixture-mode compose e2e and live external Claude Code provider smoke are verified.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.dispatch-policy: Workers dispatch harness implementations by `harness_name`; adding Claude Code-only worker services is out of contract, and Codex is not supported in the first container-local provider iteration.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.concurrency-policy: Admission control is configured per harness, for example by `harness_name` to max active runs, queue weighting, or lease budget.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.claude-code-provider-policy: Claude Code first-iteration provider configuration is container-local and env-driven: `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_PROVIDER_API_KEY` or its file variant, `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_BASE_URL`, and `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_MODEL` mirror the existing `cglm` contract and are rendered into Claude Code settings JSON as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_API_KEY`, `CLAUDE_API_BASE_URL`, and model override env values. `AGENT_RUNNER_CLAUDE_CODE_AUTH_TOKEN` remains a compatibility fallback only. Raw credentials remain worker-local and never enter control-plane payloads.
AgentRunnerConfigSurface.artifact-boundary: Full logs, prompts, tool traces, and large outputs are stored as artifacts or private request objects; database payloads keep normalized summary fields, redacted metadata, and opaque request references only.

service.name: postgres
service.role: Persistent system of record for jobs, execution claims, artifacts, events, submissions, and webhook deliveries

service.name: redis
service.role: Backing store for asynq task queues only

service.name: minio
service.role: Object store for uploaded sources and produced artifacts

service.name: minio-init
service.role: Bucket bootstrap for sources and artifacts buckets

service.name: api
service.role: Go control plane exposing REST and WebSocket

service.name: worker-transcription
service.role: Python transcription execution service including ffmpeg and faster-whisper

service.name: worker-agent-runner
service.role: Python generic agent execution service for report and deep-research operations using container-local Claude Code provider settings

service.name: telegram-bot
service.role: Thin Telegram adapter over the HTTP API

service.name: mcp-server
service.role: Thin MCP adapter over the HTTP API

network-assumptions.item: services communicate on a private Docker network
web is published to localhost with WEB_HOST_PORT override support defaulting to 3201 so it does not collide with another local service on 3000
MinIO S3 endpoint is published to localhost for presigned download use while service-to-service object IO uses Docker-internal DNS
MinIO admin console is published to localhost
telegram-bot and mcp-server call api by internal service DNS name

RuntimeTopology.whisper-mode: CPU-only; no CUDA or GPU runtime assumptions in v1

dep.name: aiogram
dep.purpose: Current Telegram bot runtime; later becomes thin adapter logic.

dep.name: faster-whisper
dep.purpose: CPU-only transcription engine reused inside Python workers.

dep.name: python-docx
dep.purpose: Transcript and report DOCX artifact rendering.

dep.name: python-dotenv
dep.purpose: Local environment loading for Python runtime components.

dep.name: youtube-transcript-api
dep.purpose: Fast subtitle retrieval path for YouTube sources.

dep.name: claude-code
dep.purpose: Container-local Claude Code CLI used by worker-agent-runner with Anthropic-compatible provider settings for the first AI provider iteration.

dep.name: net/http
dep.purpose: Go HTTP routing layer for the control plane.

dep.name: pgx
dep.purpose: Go PostgreSQL access layer through database/sql.

dep.name: minio-go
dep.purpose: Go MinIO/S3 artifact and source object access.

dep.name: goose-style-sql
dep.purpose: Database migrations for the Go API, applied by the runtime bootstrap path.

dep.name: React
dep.purpose: Local browser UI for submission, monitoring, and admin actions.

dep.name: Vite
dep.purpose: Web UI development and build pipeline.

dep.name: React Router
dep.purpose: Simple page routing for the local Web UI.

dep.name: Vitest
dep.purpose: Web UI and MCP adapter packet-local tests.

dep.name: MCP SDK
dep.purpose: Current MCP adapter uses a packet-local TypeScript shell and API client until shared SDK extraction is justified.

constraint.reason: Current repository and workers are designed around Python 3.12.
apps/api/go.mod and compose runtime use Go 1.26.
React and Vite workspace should run on one consistent local Node runtime.

StorageAndQueueContracts.postgres: Persistent authority for submissions, sources, source sets, batch drafts, jobs, worker execution claims, job artifacts, job events, and webhook deliveries.
StorageAndQueueContracts.redis: Ephemeral infrastructure for asynq only; never authoritative job state and not used as an application event bus in v1.
StorageAndQueueContracts.minio: Object storage for uploaded source files and produced artifacts; clients do not upload directly in v1.
StorageAndQueueContracts.asynq-rule: Do not place full mutable job metadata in Redis or queue payloads; workers resolve authoritative state through API-owned contracts.
StorageAndQueueContracts.source-set-model: Single-file transcription jobs use one-item source sets; batch transcription roots use `input_kind=batch_transcription` with stable source labels and per-source child source sets; `agent_run` uses a request-only source-set shell with no source items.
StorageAndQueueContracts.batch-artifact-model: Batch aggregate roots produce the required transcript artifact set plus `source_manifest_json` and `batch_diagnostics_json`; source children produce per-source transcript artifacts that the aggregate task consumes in source-label order.
StorageAndQueueContracts.batch-draft-model: Pre-submit batch drafts are durable API records, not Redis, Telegram callback text, or adapter memory. Draft mutation uses optimistic version checks; submitted, expired, or canceled drafts cannot be mutated; upload item body bytes are stored by the API into the source object store during add-item and removed from the returned draft item payload; submit is idempotent and returns the existing promoted root job when replayed. Telegram serializes per-owner draft mutation and summary scheduling so concurrent forwarded single-source updates join one API draft instead of racing into separate drafts. Locale for command copy, delayed summaries, and media-group flushes remains transient adapter presentation state and is not persisted in draft rows.

DeliveryModel.websocket-role: WebSocket is the live-update channel for Web UI only and does not replace polling correctness.
DeliveryModel.webhook-role: Webhook is a per-job delivery strategy selected at creation time and requires a callback URL.
DeliveryModel.download-model: Artifact reads return MinIO presigned URLs from the API; clients never construct bucket paths themselves. The public route `/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}` uses `MINIO_PUBLIC_ENDPOINT` for browser and host clients, while the internal route `/internal/v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/download-access` uses `MINIO_ENDPOINT` for server-side adapters and workers inside the compose or service network.

worker-agent-runner-isolation.forbidden-contract: host-side broker execution as the primary path
host path remapping for worker workspaces

baseline-python.focus: Host-side regression checks that still matter after the local service shell and compatibility re-exports were removed.

baseline-full.focus: Current regression gate that must remain conceptually preserved during migration.

target-cross-stack.focus: Target-state cross-stack validation now that Go API, Python workers, Web UI, and adapters exist.

Testing.contract-scope: OpenAPI validation, schema validation for WebSocket and webhook envelopes, and generated client compatibility.
Testing.mocking-policy: Prefer deterministic fakes, monkeypatching, and narrow subprocess or storage adapters over broad end-to-end mocks. Queue, storage, and webhook boundaries must have contract-level tests.
Testing.batch-draft-evidence: API-owned batch draft evidence is refreshed by `go test ./internal/storage ./internal/api ./internal/jobs`, `go test -count=1 ./internal/storage ./internal/api ./internal/jobs`, `uv run pytest --no-cov packages/contracts/tests/test_contract_surfaces.py`, `PYTHONPATH=apps/telegram-bot/src:workers/common/src:src uv run pytest --no-cov tests/test_bot.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_api_client.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_gateway.py`, and the compose API-container PostgreSQL E2E `TestApiBatchDraftMixedUploadURLPostgresE2E` with `API_STORAGE_POSTGRES_DSN=postgresql://telegram_transcriber:telegram_transcriber@postgres:5432/telegram_transcriber?sslmode=disable`.
Testing.telegram-i18n-evidence: Telegram RU/EN locale coverage is refreshed by `PYTHONPATH=apps/telegram-bot/src:workers/common/src:src uv run pytest --no-cov tests/test_bot.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_i18n_catalogs.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_i18n_service.py -q` reporting `32 passed in 1.33s` and by the broader adapter command `PYTHONPATH=apps/telegram-bot/src:workers/common/src:src uv run pytest --no-cov tests/test_bot.py tests/test_media_groups.py tests/test_main.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_api_client.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_gateway.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_i18n_catalogs.py apps/telegram-bot/tests/test_i18n_service.py -q` reporting `46 passed in 1.71s`.
Testing.known-limitation: Telegram adapter tests launched from the repository root still require explicit `PYTHONPATH=apps/telegram-bot/src:workers/common/src:src`; this GRACE refresh does not change root pytest configuration.

Observability.logger: Python stdlib logging currently; target state is structured logs in the Go API and Python workers with stable marker fields.
Observability.required-fields: jobId, rootJobId, sourceId, workerKind, attemptNo, deliveryStrategy
Observability.redaction: Never log bot tokens, secrets, presigned URLs, raw high-risk payloads, or full unbounded prompt bodies.

DeliveryShape.shape: Single local deployment with one PostgreSQL instance, one Redis instance, one MinIO instance, one Go API control plane, separate Python workers, one React UI, one Telegram adapter, and one MCP adapter.
DeliveryShape.preserved-capabilities: YouTube transcription, Telegram voice or audio or video or document transcription, media groups, plain transcript, segmented transcript, report generation, deep research generation, and mixed-source batch transcription through one aggregate root job.

PreservationPolicy.rule-1: This technology artifact preserves the target stack, monorepo shape, runtime topology, storage model, queue model, environment variables, and delivery assumptions from the original migration plan.
PreservationPolicy.rule-2: Detailed endpoint, schema, data-model, wave, and verification content are intentionally expanded in the GRACE development and verification artifacts rather than omitted here.
